   <item>
      <title>Pelicans Identify Darvin Ham, Steve Hetzel, Rajon Rondo, Sean Sweeney As Coach Finalists</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285469/Pelicans-Identify-Darvin-Ham-Steve-Hetzel-Rajon-Rondo-Sean-Sweeney-As-Coach-Finalists</link>
      <description><p>The New Orleans Pelicans have reportedly identified four candidates as finalists for their open head coach vacancy. The finals are Milwaukee Bucks assistant coaches Darvin Ham and Rajon Rondo, Brooklyn Nets assistant coach Steve Hetzel and San Antonio Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney.</p>
<p>This list does not include James Borrego, who finished the season as the Pelicans interim head coach. Borrego took over when New Orleans fired Willie Green after 12 games. New Orleans finished 24-46 under Borrego to end the season.</p>
<p>The Pelicans could also add Jamahl Mosley to the list of candidates. Mosley was recently fired after five seasons as the head coach of the Orlando Magic. New Orleans has been reported to be fans of Mosley for several months now, in anticipation that he would be let go by Orlando.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 09:22:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285469/Pelicans-Identify-Darvin-Ham-Steve-Hetzel-Rajon-Rondo-Sean-Sweeney-As-Coach-Finalists</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_neworleanspelicans.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bryson Graham Given Full Autonomy By Bulls To Rebuild Front Office</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285473/Bryson-Graham-Given-Full-Autonomy-By-Bulls-To-Rebuild-Front-Office</link>
      <description><p>The Chicago Bulls selected Bryson Graham as their new executive vice president of basketball operations over several high-profile candidates, with team sources telling ESPN the 39-year-old stood out for his range of experience and ability to unify a front office widely viewed as disjointed under the previous regime.</p>
<p>Matt Lloyd, who spent 17 seasons with the Bulls across multiple roles from 1995 to 2012, had been considered the frontrunner league-wide due to his organizational ties. Graham ultimately won out, with sources citing his diverse career background as a key factor in the decision.</p>
<p>Chicago is expected to grant Graham broad autonomy in restructuring the front office, sources told ESPN. That flexibility was a deliberate choice by CEO Michael Reinsdorf, who hired a search firm and conducted a thorough process that included interviews with Mike Gansey of the Cleveland Cavaliers, Dave Telep of the San Antonio Spurs and Dave Lewin of the Boston Celtics.</p>
<p>Graham now inherits a roster that includes Josh Giddey, Matas Buzelis, Tre Jones and Noa Essengue, who missed nearly all of last season following left shoulder surgery. Finding a head coach to replace Billy Donovan, who stepped down after six seasons, is among his first priorities.</p>
<p>The Bulls also enter the offseason with significant resources. Chicago holds its own 2026 lottery pick, Portland's 2026 first-round selection and nearly $60 million in cap space, giving Graham a strong foundation heading into a deep draft class.</p>
<p>Chicago finished 31-51 this season, missing the playoffs for the fourth straight year.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 10:17:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285473/Bryson-Graham-Given-Full-Autonomy-By-Bulls-To-Rebuild-Front-Office</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_chicagobulls.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tom Thibodeau Eyes Coaching Comeback, Would Consider Return To Bulls</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285475/Tom-Thibodeau-Eyes-Coaching-Comeback-Would-Consider-Return-To-Bulls</link>
      <description><p>Tom Thibodeau is actively pursuing a return to coaching and has expressed strong interest in re-entering the NBA, with league sources telling The Athletic that the 68-year-old is open to a reunion with the Chicago Bulls if the organization shares that interest.</p>
<p>Thibodeau spent the 2025-26 season away from basketball after the New York Knicks dismissed him last summer, despite the team reaching the Eastern Conference Finals. The Bulls job would carry particular significance, as Chicago is where Thibodeau built his head coaching reputation before departing in 2015.</p>
<p>Thibodeau's track record suggests a short absence from the sidelines before returning. Following his exits from the Bulls in 2015 and the Minnesota Timberwolves in 2019, Thibodeau returned to coaching within one season each time.</p>
<p>Any potential Bulls reunion would require buy-in from CEO Michael Reinsdorf and newly hired executive vice president of basketball operations Bryson Graham, who joined the franchise Monday after being hired away from the Atlanta Hawks.</p>
<p>Thibodeau is one of several coaches expected to draw interest across a wide-open market this offseason. Current vacancies include the Bulls, Orlando Magic, Portland Trail Blazers and New Orleans Pelicans. The Golden State Warriors also faces uncertainty, with Steve Kerr still deciding whether to continue coaching.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 10:24:05 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285475/Tom-Thibodeau-Eyes-Coaching-Comeback-Would-Consider-Return-To-Bulls</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Thibodeau_Tom_nyk_230502.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Raptors Pursued Jaren Jackson Jr. Trade At Deadline But Couldn't Move Jakob Poeltl's $104M Deal</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285476/Raptors-Pursued-Jaren-Jackson-Jr-Trade-At-Deadline-But-Couldnt-Move-Jakob-Poeltls-$104M-Deal</link>
      <description><p>The Toronto Raptors made a serious push to acquire Jaren Jackson Jr. from the Memphis Grizzlies before the February trade deadline, but were unable to complete a deal due to the burden of Jakob Poeltl's remaining contract, according to multiple league sources.</p>
<p>Memphis ultimately sent Jackson to the Utah Jazz in a package that included three first-round picks. Toronto's pursuit stalled over its inability to find a taker for Poeltl's four-year, $104 million commitment.</p>
<p>The 30-year-old Austrian center was sidelined for an extended stretch with back problems at the time of the deadline, making his contract one of the least attractive in the league. Interest from other teams was minimal.</p>
<p>Poeltl's availability improved after the All-Star break and he played through the postseason without issue. However, his performance against the Cleveland Cavaliers in the first round raised fresh concerns, as he appeared overmatched in his first playoff appearance in six seasons.</p>
<p>The failed Jackson pursuit illustrates the broader challenge facing general manager Bobby Webster this offseason. Several large contracts, including Immanuel Quickley's four years and $130 million and Brandon Ingram's two years and $82 million, continue to limit Toronto's ability to reshape its roster. The Raptors signed Poeltl to his extension just last offseason.</p>
<p>The Raptors also pursued a trade for Anthony Davis with the Dallas Mavericks, but the required salaries they would have to send out made it challenging to execute. Davis was instead traded to the Washington Wizards for expiring contracts.</p>
<p>The Raptors finished the regular season 46-11 before falling to the Cavaliers in seven games, with Scottie Barnes averaging 24.1 points, 8.6 assists and 6.1 rebounds in the series.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 10:30:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285476/Raptors-Pursued-Jaren-Jackson-Jr-Trade-At-Deadline-But-Couldnt-Move-Jakob-Poeltls-$104M-Deal</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jackson_Jaren_uta_260212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bulls Pursuing Celtics Assistant GM Dave Lewin For Front Office Role</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285477/Bulls-Pursuing-Celtics-Assistant-GM-Dave-Lewin-For-Front-Office-Role</link>
      <description><p>Although Dave Lewin did not land the Chicago Bulls' executive vice president of basketball operations position, the organization intends to pursue the Boston Celtics assistant general manager for a front office role.</p>
<p>Lewin's interview left a strong impression. One source described the internal push to bring him aboard in clear terms: "We need to find someone very much like Dave Lewin" if the Bulls cannot secure him.</p>
<p>Lewin was one of three finalists for the lead position that ultimately went to Bryson Graham, who was hired away from the Atlanta Hawks on Monday. Lewin's presentation stood out specifically for its depth in analytics and organizational strategy.</p>
<p>Currently behind Brad Stevens and Mike Zarren within the Celtics' front office, Lewin built his reputation with the Celtics over nearly a decade. He joined the organization as a scout before becoming director of player personnel in 2019 and was promoted to assistant general manager in 2023.</p>
<p>For Graham, landing Lewin would provide immediate analytical support as he works to restructure a front office widely described as fractured under the previous regime. Chicago has granted Graham broad autonomy to build out his staff as he sees fit.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 11:37:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285477/Bulls-Pursuing-Celtics-Assistant-GM-Dave-Lewin-For-Front-Office-Role</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_chicagobulls.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>WNBA GMs Choose Paige Bueckers Over Caitlin Clark, A'ja Wilson As Franchise Building Block</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285483/WNBA-GMs-Choose-Paige-Bueckers-Over-Caitlin-Clark-Aja-Wilson-As-Franchise-Building-Block</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Wings guard Paige Bueckers received the most votes from WNBA general managers as the player they would select first if starting a franchise today, the league's annual GM survey revealed Tuesday, with Las Vegas Aces star A'ja Wilson and Indiana Fever guard Caitlin Clark each drawing 20% support compared to Bueckers' 33%.</p>
<p>The result mirrors last year's survey, when Clark earned the same distinction coming off her Rookie of the Year season. Bueckers is now in that same position after her own rookie campaign. Clark, 24, was limited to 13 games last season due to injury. Wilson is 29.</p>
<p>Wilson dominated the individual categories despite finishing second in the franchise poll. The four-time MVP drew 60% of votes as the favorite to win the 2026 MVP award, ahead of New York Liberty forward Breanna Stewart (27%) and Clark and Atlanta Dream guard Allisha Gray at 7% each. Wilson also led voting as the league's top defender (53%), the player forcing the most opposing adjustments (60%) and the best leader (79%).</p>
<p>Wilson's Aces, the reigning 2025 WNBA champions, received 40% of votes as the title favorite this season. The Liberty followed at 33% and the Atlanta Dream at 27%.</p>
<p>Las Vegas teammate Chelsea Gray swept several skill categories, leading votes for best point guard (73%), best passer (93%), best basketball IQ (93%) and most trusted player for a winning shot (36%).</p>
<p>Minnesota Lynx guard Olivia Miles drew 73% support for Rookie of the Year over Dallas' Azzi Fudd at 20%. However, Seattle Storm forward Awa Fam earned 67% of votes as the rookie projected to be the best player in five years.</p>
<p>Minnesota coach Cheryl Reeve was voted best head coach at 53%, ahead of Las Vegas' Becky Hammon at 40%. The WNBA opens its 30th season Friday.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 17:48:38 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285483/WNBA-GMs-Choose-Paige-Bueckers-Over-Caitlin-Clark-Aja-Wilson-As-Franchise-Building-Block</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/wnba_dallaswings.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joe Mazzulla Expected To Remain With Celtics, Win NBA Coach of the Year</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285482/Joe-Mazzulla-Expected-To-Remain-With-Celtics-Win-NBA-Coach-of-the-Year</link>
      <description><p>Joe Mazzulla is expected to return as Boston Celtics head coach next season and is anticipated to be named NBA Coach of the Year in the coming days, despite the franchise's stunning first-round playoff elimination at the hands of the seventh-seeded Philadelphia 76ers.</p>
<p>Mazzulla's team became <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Boston-Celtics/2/playoff-history">the first in franchise history to surrender a 3-1 series lead</a>, falling to a Joel Embiid-led Philadelphia club that won three consecutive games to advance. Despite the collapse, all indications point to Mazzulla remaining on the bench as the organization turns its attention to roster decisions this offseason.</p>
<p>The Celtics enter the summer in sound financial shape after their significant maneuvers in 2025. Boston sits below both apron thresholds and the luxury-tax line, giving the front office access to the $15 million non-taxpayer midlevel exception and the $5.5 million biannual exception. The Celtics also hold trade exceptions of $27.7 million, $8.4 million and $4.7 million, along with the 27th and 40th picks in next month's draft.</p>
<p>Franchise cornerstones Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown headline the returning salary commitments at $58.5 million and $57.1 million respectively. </p>
<p>Nikola Vucevic, who struggled with a broken finger after arriving via trade in February, is Boston's only unrestricted free agent. The 35-year-old center made $21.5 million this season and will be playing under a greatly reduced salary next season.</p>
<p>Center Neemias Queta, who finished fourth in Most Improved Player voting, will have his team option exercised. Guard Jordan Walsh is also expected to have his option picked up.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 14:55:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285482/Joe-Mazzulla-Expected-To-Remain-With-Celtics-Win-NBA-Coach-of-the-Year</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mazzulla_Joe_bos_240606.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Taylor Jenkins Among NBA's Highest-Paid Coaches At Over $10M Per Year With Bucks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285471/Taylor-Jenkins-Among-NBAs-Highest-Paid-Coaches-At-Over-$10M-Per-Year-With-Bucks</link>
      <description><p>Taylor Jenkins has signed a long-term contract worth well above $10 million annually to become the next head coach of the Milwaukee Bucks, per league sources, making him one of the highest-paid coaches in the NBA.</p>
<p>Jenkins ranks amongst the NBA's highest paid head coaches, behind the likes of Steve Kerr, who earns $17.5 million per year with the Golden State Warriors, and Erik Spoelstra, who makes approximately $15 million annually with the Miami Heat. Doug Christie is the NBA's lowest paid coach at $2 million per season.</p>
<p>Jenkins replaces Doc Rivers, who was dismissed following the 2025-26 season. The 44-year-old spent the 2025-26 season away from basketball after the Memphis Grizzlies let him go near the end of the 2024-25 regular season.</p>
<p>Jenkins brings direct familiarity with the Bucks organization. He served as an assistant under Mike Budenholzer during the 2018-19 season, working alongside Giannis Antetokounmpo during the forward's rise to superstardom.</p>
<p>The hire comes as Milwaukee faces an uncertain summer centered on Antetokounmpo's future with the franchise. Team officials moved quickly to fill the coaching vacancy with an established candidate.</p>
<p>Jenkins went on to lead Memphis for six seasons after leaving Milwaukee, building the Grizzlies into a competitive playoff program before his dismissal.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 10:00:26 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285471/Taylor-Jenkins-Among-NBAs-Highest-Paid-Coaches-At-Over-$10M-Per-Year-With-Bucks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jenkins_Taylor_mem_220429.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Mike Brown's Offensive Vision 'Starting To Click' With Another Dominant Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285479/Mike-Browns-Offensive-Vision-Starting-To-Click-With-Another-Dominant-Game</link>
      <description><p>The New York Knicks appear to be hitting their stride at the right moment, with the offensive system coach Mike Brown envisioned when he replaced Tom Thibodeau last summer now operating at historically efficient levels through their first seven playoff games.</p>
<p>New York shot 53-of-84 from the field in Monday's 137-98 blowout of the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 1 of the second round. The numbers over the past three games are striking: the Knicks posted 1.339 points per possession in Game 5 against Atlanta, 1.435 in Game 6, and 1.431 in Game 1 against Philadelphia. For context, the Denver Nuggets led the NBA in offensive rating this season at 1.226 points per possession.</p>
<p>New York has outscored opponents by 135 points dating back to that Game 4 against the Hawks, topping 125 points in three of those four contests.</p>
<p>Brown's system was designed to eliminate predictability through connected concepts rather than rigid play calls, allowing players to flow from one action to the next until an advantage emerged.</p>
<p>"It's a lot of fun when you have concepts on either end of the floor and guys are trying to embrace those concepts at the highest level," Brown said. "When they do, it doesn't really matter what you call or initiates the action because guys are trying to play the right way. It can be any team out on the floor. If they're trying to embrace what you're throwing out there, sacrificing, all of that other stuff, it can be a lot of fun to watch."</p>
<p>The results were slow to develop. New York reverted to more familiar offensive sets early in the season to accommodate its personnel. Now, with each starter operating within defined roles, the pieces are connecting.</p>
<p>Jalen Brunson scored 35 points on 12-of-18 shooting. Karl-Anthony Towns controlled the pinch post, creating looks for cutters and shooters. OG Anunoby finished with 18 points on 7-of-8 shooting, punishing single coverage. Every starter except Josh Hart scored at least 17 points. New York recorded 34 assists.</p>
<p>"We're being unselfish," Hart said. "When we do that, we're playing our best basketball."</p>
<p>"You always want to peak later in the season, so this is the process of the year," said Anunoby. "We're starting to click, and hopefully getting better and better. We're a really tough team with threats all over the place. It's very hard to guard."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 14:31:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285479/Mike-Browns-Offensive-Vision-Starting-To-Click-With-Another-Dominant-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Mike_nyk_251025.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Gradey Dick Open To Trade From Raptors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285470/Gradey-Dick-Open-To-Trade-From-Raptors</link>
      <description><p>Toronto Raptors wing Gradey Dick's third NBA season didn't go the way he had hoped. Despite playing in a career-high 76 games, Dick turned in his worst NBA season. His role in the Raptors rotation was reduced as the season went along, as Dick finished with the worst stats of his three-year career.</p>
<p>Toronto may look to trade Dick this offseason, as a way to free up flexibility under the luxury tax. A move to another team is something that the 6-foot-7 wing is open to. Dick is set to make $7.1 million next season in the final year of his rookie scale contract. He'll be extension-eligible this offseason, and a potential restricted free agent in 2027 if no extension agreement is reached.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 09:34:53 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285470/Gradey-Dick-Open-To-Trade-From-Raptors</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dick_Gradey_tor_241028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Edwards Returns From Knee Injury Early For Wolves' Game 1 Win Over Spurs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285468/Anthony-Edwards-Returns-From-Knee-Injury-Early-For-Wolves-Game-1-Win-Over-Spurs</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Edwards returned from a bone bruise ahead of schedule, coming off the bench to score 18 points in the Minnesota Timberwolves' 104-102 road victory over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the Western Conference semifinals Monday night.</p>
<p>Edwards suffered the deep bone bruise eight days earlier when his left knee bent sharply on a block attempt. Team sources had told ESPN he was trending toward a Game 3 return, once the series moved to Minneapolis. His own coach, Chris Finch, said the earliest he had expected Edwards to play was Game 2.</p>
<p>"Nobody expected him to play," Mike Conley said.</p>
<p>Edwards pushed that timeline forward himself. He told the team's medical staff and front office his knee had responded well enough to justify an earlier return. He appeared at Monday morning's shootaround wearing a thick sleeve on his left knee and was cleared to play.</p>
<p>The Timberwolves limited Edwards to 25 minutes as a precaution. He delivered 18 points on 8-of-13 shooting, including 11 points in the final nine minutes to help close out the narrow win.</p>
<p>"I know for a fact, just me being out there, it calms everybody down," Edwards said. "Not saying there's any pressure on any of my teammates, but it takes pressure off of everybody just knowing that I'm out there, I'm available to play, yeah. And just doing what I do best, just trying to put the ball in the hoop."</p>
<p>Minnesota's defense was equally decisive. The Timberwolves held De'Aaron Fox and Victor Wembanyama to a combined 10-of-31 shooting. Wembanyama set an NBA playoff record with 12 blocks, but San Antonio still fell short. Julian Champagnie's buzzer-beating 3-point attempt missed, securing the win.</p>
<p>"We have to be better," Wembanyama said. "It shows up on the stat sheet. We need to figure out before 48 hours what we can do better and I've got no doubt that we will. I trust us."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/boxscore/2026-05-04/Minnesota-at-San-Antonio/523015">The Wolves scored 110.8 points per 100 possessions compared to 108.7 for the Spurs in a 94 possession game</a>.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/best-nba-bets/">The Spurs are 9.5 point favorites for Game 2</a> on Wednesday at Frost Bank Center in San Antonio. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 07:39:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285468/Anthony-Edwards-Returns-From-Knee-Injury-Early-For-Wolves-Game-1-Win-Over-Spurs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Edwards_Anthony_min_250516.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jason Kidd Has Four Years Remaining On Contract With Mavs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285467/Jason-Kidd-Has-Four-Years-Remaining-On-Contract-With-Mavs</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Mavericks head coach Jason Kidd has four years remaining on his contract, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>Kidd's contract has been extended each of the past two seasons as the Knicks and Lakers expressed interest in hiring him.</p>
<p>The <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285451/Masai-Ujiri-Hired-By-Mavericks-As-Team-President-After-Six-Month-Search">Mavs hired Masai Ujiri</a> as the team's new lead executive on Monday.</p>
<p>It has been said that Mavs owner Patrick Dumont was looking for an exec who would keep Kidd as the head coach due to his strong relationship with Cooper Flagg.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 02:08:08 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285467/Jason-Kidd-Has-Four-Years-Remaining-On-Contract-With-Mavs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kidd_Jason_dal_240606.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Patrick Dumont Wanted Lead Executive With Proven Track Record, Willingness To Be Communicative</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285466/Patrick-Dumont-Wanted-Lead-Executive-With-Proven-Track-Record-Willingness-To-Be-Communicative</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Mavericks owner Patrick Dumont was looking for a lead executive with a proven track record and a willingness to be communicative to the public, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>The <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285451/Masai-Ujiri-Hired-By-Mavericks-As-Team-President-After-Six-Month-Search">Mavs hired Masai Ujiri</a> on Monday.</p>
<p>Ujiri's visibility while with Toronto was important due to Dumont's reticence to speak publicly.</p>
<p>The search for a lead exec was taken on by Dumont, CEO Rick Welts and president of business operations Ethan Casson.</p>
<p>Dumont had multiple conversations with Bob Myers throughout the process before eventually hiring Ujiri. </p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Karl-Anthony Towns Keeps Knicks Focused After 39-Point Blowout Game 1 Win Over 76ers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285464/Karl-Anthony-Towns-Keeps-Knicks-Focused-After-39-Point-Blowout-Game-1-Win-Over-76ers</link>
      <description><p>The New York Knicks defeated the Philadelphia 76ers 137-98 in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference semifinals on Monday night.</p>
<p>The Knicks became the first team in NBA history to win three straight postseason games by at least 25 points. </p>
<p>Jalen Brunson scored 35 points in the win, with OG Anunoby adding 18 points. Karl-Anthony Towns and Mikal Bridges both had 17.</p>
<p>"We're playing well, but it doesn't mean anything if we can't find a way to get three more wins," Towns said after the game. "So, we've just got to stick to the task at hand."</p>
<p>The Knicks have now won their last three games by a total of 119 points, the largest differential over three games in playoff history.</p>
<p>"They're trying to focus on the details no matter what the score is," Knicks coach Mike Brown said. "They're still trying to stay locked in. And it shows by, you know, whether we're up double digits or not, we're still diving on the floor for loose balls. We're still trying to get out in transition and get to the corners to put pressure on the defense. You know, we're still trying to play the right way."</p>
<p>"That's why you play a season," Brunson said. "That's why you go through adversity. You find things to make you the best team as possible by the end of the year. And even at this point, you find ways to get better and improve."</p>
<p>Paul George scored 17 points for the Sixers, with Joel Embiid adding 14 points. </p>
<p>"They were obviously picking us apart, moving a lot better than we were," 76ers coach Nick Nurse said.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 2 takes place on Wednesday night at Madison Square Garden with the Knicks favored by 6.5 points</a>. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 05 May 2026 01:36:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285464/Karl-Anthony-Towns-Keeps-Knicks-Focused-After-39-Point-Blowout-Game-1-Win-Over-76ers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Towns_Karlanthony_nyk_260420.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jeff Weltman Vows To Keep Magic Core Intact Despite First-Round Collapse</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285463/Jeff-Weltman-Vows-To-Keep-Magic-Core-Intact-Despite-First-Round-Collapse</link>
      <description><p>Orlando Magic president Jeff Weltman said Monday the franchise has no plans to dismantle its roster following a first-round playoff collapse against the Detroit Pistons, pointing to the team's performance before Franz Wagner's injury as evidence the core remains capable of competing.</p>
<p>"When healthy, we were top five [on] defense and top 10 [on] offense," Weltman said. "I don't want to tear this thing down and dismantle it because of the way it ended and certainly one half."</p>
<p>Wagner, limited to 34 games during the regular season, suffered a strained right calf in Game 4 against the Pistons. Orlando lost all three remaining games after his exit, including surrendering a 24-point third-quarter lead in Game 6.</p>
<p>"Had we stayed healthy, I'd like to imagine where we would have gone in this series and beyond," Weltman said. "I don't see any reason why we couldn't have competed against anyone else in the East if we could compete against the No. 1 team like that."</p>
<p>Weltman acknowledged the collapse will shape how the organization evaluates the offseason but stressed that injuries, not roster construction, drove the outcome.</p>
<p>"It's very frustrating," Weltman said. "But it's also very encouraging because whenever they are together, they have been elite."</p>
<p>Orlando traded four unprotected first-round picks, a swap, Kentavious Caldwell-Pope and Cole Anthony last offseason to acquire Desmond Bane. Weltman's comments suggest that investment remains the foundation of the team's path forward.</p>
<p>The Magic are searching for a new head coach to replace Jamahl Mosley. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 04 May 2026 17:53:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285463/Jeff-Weltman-Vows-To-Keep-Magic-Core-Intact-Despite-First-Round-Collapse</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Weltman_Jeff_orl_170628.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bryson Graham Hired By Bulls As Executive Vice President Of Basketball Operations</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285462/Bryson-Graham-Hired-By-Bulls-As-Executive-Vice-President-Of-Basketball-Operations</link>
      <description><p>Bryson Graham has been hired by the Chicago Bulls as Executive Vice President of Basketball Operations. Graham was most recently senior vice president for the Atlanta Hawks.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/staff/Bryson-Graham/Summary/1161">Graham had spent 15 seasons with the New Orleans Pelicans before joining the Hawks last offseason</a>.</p>
<p>The Bulls also considered a number of other candidates for the job with Matt Lloyd of the Minnesota Timberwolves considered the frontrunner at one point. Dennis Lindsey was the other finalist for the job before Michael Reinsdorf and the Bulls moved forward with Graham.</p>
<p>Graham will replace Arturas Karnisovas running the front office of the Bulls as they continue their rebuild. </p>
<p>"I am incredibly honored to join the Chicago Bulls organization," said Graham. "This is one of the most storied franchises in the history of professional basketball, and I feel a tremendous sense of responsibility to deliver results for this city and these fans. My entire career has been built on the belief that sustained success starts with finding the right players and developing an all-around impactful culture. I want to thank Jerry and Michael Reinsdorf and the entire Bulls organization for presenting me with this opportunity. I am ready to get to work."</p>
<p>"We're excited to name Bryson Graham as our Executive Vice President of Basketball Operations," Reinsdorf said. "Bryson is an elite talent evaluator who has earned tremendous respect across the league, and that stood out immediately during our process. He has worked his way up through basketball operations from the ground level, and that experience has given him a deep understanding of how to build and sustain a successful organization. He is an effective communicator, a disciplined and thoughtful decision-maker, and someone who truly connects with players and people.</p>
<p>"He understands today's league, today's players, and what it takes to develop talent and build a winning culture. Just as important, Bryson is committed to building a high-level group around him. He knows what he does well, and he is focused on surrounding that with strong leadership across strategy, scouting and player development. This is an important step for our organization. We know there is work ahead, but we are confident in Bryson's ability to lead, build and move us forward."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 04 May 2026 16:55:52 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285462/Bryson-Graham-Hired-By-Bulls-As-Executive-Vice-President-Of-Basketball-Operations</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_chicagobulls.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Kevin Durant, Ime Udoka Remain Part Of Rockets' Plans</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285459/Kevin-Durant-Ime-Udoka-Remain-Part-Of-Rockets-Plans</link>
      <description><p>Kevin Durant and head coach Ime Udoka are both expected to remain with the Houston Rockets despite a first-round playoff elimination. However, team sources tell ESPN the franchise views its young core as the foundation of a decade-long contention window rather than a supporting cast built around Durant's timeline.</p>
<p>"We aren't thinking, 'We've got to win now because we're in KD's window,'" a high-ranking team source told ESPN. "We are hyperfocused on our young core. Our five guys have a chance to win a lot of games together for a long time. We don't want to make the mistake other teams have of giving up on guys too soon. We want a 10-year run."</p>
<p>Durant, 37, averaged 26.0 points, 5.5 rebounds and 4.8 assists across 78 regular season games this season. He holds a two-year contract extension that includes a $46 million player option for 2027-28. Knee and ankle injuries limited him to partial duty in Houston's six-game series loss to the Lakers, where he was unable to return after Game 2.</p>
<p>His absence exposed the team's offensive limitations. Udoka acknowledged the gap Durant's injuries created.</p>
<p>"It's nights like this where guys are struggling, you want a 25-, 26-point scorer with his efficiency and the way he does it to avoid some of these nights when other guys are struggling," Udoka said after the elimination loss.</p>
<p>The partnership, however, carried off-court complications. Team sources told ESPN that Durant's moodiness wore on Houston's younger players throughout the season, a dynamic worsened by the absence of VanVleet and Steven Adams, whose season also ended due to injury, as stabilizing influences in the locker room.</p>
<p>Those tensions grew more pronounced following allegations that Durant had criticized Alperen Sengun and Jabari Smith Jr. through a burner account on X during All-Star weekend. Durant addressed teammates in what sources described as a team discussion rather than a formal meeting, and the matter moved forward without a public resolution.</p>
<p>Multiple high-level team sources told ESPN that Udoka's role is also secure heading into next season. Houston's priorities this offseason include improving three-point shooting, the team ranked 24th in makes and 28th in attempts, negotiating a new contract with restricted free agent Tari Eason and determining a path forward with VanVleet and Adams, both 32, returning from significant injuries.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 04 May 2026 15:20:16 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285459/Kevin-Durant-Ime-Udoka-Remain-Part-Of-Rockets-Plans</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Durant_Kevin_hou_251010.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Rockets Passed On James Harden Reunion Due To Concerns On How He'd Impact Young Core</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285458/Rockets-Passed-On-James-Harden-Reunion-Due-To-Concerns-On-How-Hed-Impact-Young-Core</link>
      <description><p>Despite mutual interest and a clear need at point guard following Fred VanVleet's torn ACL, the Houston Rockets declined to pursue a reunion with James Harden this season, prioritizing the development of their young core over a short-term fix, sources told ESPN.</p>
<p>Harden's representatives contacted Houston after the Los Angeles Clippers stumbled to a 6-21 start, gauging interest in a potential return to the franchise that originally built around him before trading him to the Brooklyn Nets in January 2021.</p>
<p>VanVleet's season-ending knee injury, suffered during a players-only mini-camp in the Bahamas in late September, had left Houston without its primary playmaker and locker room anchor. </p>
<p>The Rockets declined despite the obvious need and the way Harden had maintained his All-Star status. The Rockets didn't want the presence of Harden on the floor to hinder the development of Reed Sheppard, Alperen Sengun and Amen Thompson.</p>
<p>"We're not really looking for a heliocentric player, as great as James still is," one team source told ESPN. "We want to develop Reed, we want to develop Amen and we want the ball in Alpy's hands."</p>
<p>A second source was equally direct.</p>
<p>"We weren't going to put the ball in [Harden's] hands, so why would you trade for James if you're not going to give him the ball?"</p>
<p>This was not the first time Houston passed on a Harden reunion. In 2023, the Rockets chose to sign VanVleet to a three-year, $130 million contract rather than pursue Harden after he failed to secure a maximum extension with the Philadelphia 76ers.</p>
<p>The decision to bypass Harden this season reverberated throughout the first-round playoff series against the Los Angeles Lakers. Sheppard, Thompson and Sengun each produced tantalizing moments alongside inexplicable ones, with poor shot selection and decision-making contributing directly to losses.</p>
<p>Harden was ultimately traded by the Clippers to the Cleveland Cavaliers ahead of the deadline in February. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 04 May 2026 15:11:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285458/Rockets-Passed-On-James-Harden-Reunion-Due-To-Concerns-On-How-Hed-Impact-Young-Core</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Harden_James_cle_260420.jpg" />
   </item>

      <description><p>Anthony Edwards has been cleared for Game 1 of the Minnesota Timberwolves' second round series against the San Antonio Spurs as he recovers from a bone bruise and hyperextended knee. Edwards was a significant participant during Wolves' shootaround on Monday morning.</p>

   <item>
      <title>Rockets Internally Project Reed Sheppard As Steve Nash-Level Talent</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285456/Rockets-Internally-Project-Reed-Sheppard-As-Steve-Nash-Level-Talent</link>
      <description><p>Despite a turbulent playoff run, the Houston Rockets remain firm believers in guard Reed Sheppard's long-term potential, with internal sources comparing his ceiling to Hall of Fame point guard Steve Nash, sources tell ESPN</p>
<p>Sheppard, selected third overall in the 2024 draft, struggled with consistency throughout Houston's first-round loss to the Los Angeles Lakers. He shot 16-for-64 in the Rockets' four defeats but 11-for-24 in their two wins, a split that illustrated both his promise and his volatility.</p>
<p>The inconsistency drew outside skepticism. One rival scout offered a sharply different projection when asked about the Nash comparison.</p>
<p>"More like Steve Kerr," the scout said, envisioning Sheppard as an undersized reserve rather than a primary offensive engine.</p>
<p>Internally, the Rockets push back on that assessment.</p>
<p>"He just flashes so much greatness you can't teach," a team source told ESPN. "From Steve Nash to Steph Curry, none of those little guards play great their first few years. We're still big believers in Reed."</p>
<p>Sheppard's poor defense in Game 1 limited him to 11 minutes in Game 2. He contributed a damaging turnover in Game 3's late collapse but also delivered a key steal off LeBron James in Game 5 that helped seal a road win. He finished Game 6 shooting 4-of-19, including 1-of-10 from three.</p>
<p>The Rockets drafted Sheppard one spot ahead of Stephon Castle, the 2025-26 Rookie of the Year, who is <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/player-rankings">an All-NBA caliber talent</a> in his second season with the San Antonio Spurs. Houston's belief in Sheppard's temperament to grow from this season's adversity remains unchanged heading into the offseason.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 04 May 2026 15:00:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285456/Rockets-Internally-Project-Reed-Sheppard-As-Steve-Nash-Level-Talent</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Sheppard_Reed_hou_241028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jaylen Brown Says Referees Had 'Agenda' Against Him, Calls Out Joel Embiid Flopping</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285455/Jaylen-Brown-Says-Referees-Had-Agenda-Against-Him-Calls-Out-Joel-Embiid-Flopping</link>
      <description><p>Less than 24 hours after the Boston Celtics' Game 7 loss to the Philadelphia 76ers, Jaylen Brown took to Twitch to address his team's collapse, crediting Joel Embiid's return while accusing referees of maintaining a personal agenda against him throughout the series.</p>
<p>Brown opened by acknowledging Embiid's impact after the center returned from emergency appendicitis surgery to average 28.6 points across Philadelphia's final three wins.</p>
<p>"Even though we was up 3-1, when Joel Embiid came back, it was an issue," Brown said.</p>
<p>Brown admitted he had underestimated Embiid before the series, having said publicly that Philadelphia might function better without their star center. He suggested Embiid used those comments as motivation.</p>
<p>"I give him credit," Brown said. "He came out, and he made a difference in this series, no question."</p>
<p>However, Brown stopped short of full praise, accusing Embiid of excessive flopping across the series in which the center drew 28 free-throw attempts over the final three games.</p>
<p>"I still think he flopped entirely, way too much," Brown said. "Flopping and exaggerating contact is different. It's like when somebody barely touched you and you selling a call to make it seem like you just got shot. ... Argue with your grandma, flopping has ruined our game. Joel Embiid is a great player, one of the best bigs in f-ing basketball history, flops. He knows it. This ain't breaking news."</p>
<p>Brown then directed his frustration at officiating, claiming referees targeted him specifically after he criticized officials during the regular season. Boston finished last in free-throw attempts during the regular season and 14th among 16 first-round playoff teams.</p>
<p>"Every player does it. So why are you targeting me?" Brown said. "They clearly had an agenda. Maybe because I spoke and was critical of refs in the regular season. So they responded, 'You're gonna lead the playoffs in offensive fouls.' That's how that was, the response from the officiating crew. You could clearly tell. And I've actually spoke to some refs, and they said it was an agenda going into each game. 'So anytime Jaylen brings his arm up, just from reputation, just call it.'"</p>
<p>Brown, who scored 33 points in Game 7 and averaged a career-high 28.7 points this season, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Boston-Celtics/2/playoff-history">has reached six conference finals, two NBA Finals and won the 2023-24 championship</a>. It marks the first time in his career his season has ended in a first-round series in which he was healthy enough to play.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 04 May 2026 14:22:57 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285455/Jaylen-Brown-Says-Referees-Had-Agenda-Against-Him-Calls-Out-Joel-Embiid-Flopping</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Jaylen_bos_250504.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Masai Ujiri Hired By Mavericks As Team President After Six-Month Search</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285451/Masai-Ujiri-Hired-By-Mavericks-As-Team-President-After-Six-Month-Search</link>
      <description><p>Masai Ujiri has agreed to join the Dallas Mavericks as team president and alternate governor, sources told ESPN. A news conference with Mavs governor Patrick Dumont is planned for Tuesday in Dallas. The hire ends a six-month search that began when general manager Nico Harrison was dismissed November 11.</p>
<p>Ujiri, 55, spent 15 seasons as a lead NBA executive, compiling a 690-504 record and reaching the playoffs 12 times. He architected Toronto's 2018-19 championship and won Executive of the Year honors in 2012-13 with Denver. He did not work in the NBA this season after parting ways with the Raptors following last summer's draft.</p>
<p>Dumont first met with Ujiri during a four-hour lunch in Las Vegas in December. The Mavs governor had also expressed preliminary interest in Minnesota Timerbwolves president of basketball operations Tim Connelly before moving on amid doubts the rival franchise would permit contact with the executive, who has one year remaining on his contract.</p>
<p>Dallas finished 26-56 this season, giving the franchise the eighth-best odds in the upcoming draft lottery. The hire carries urgency with the NBA draft combine beginning May 10. The Mavericks own two first-round picks in this draft, their own lottery selection and the No. 30 pick acquired from the Washington Wizards in the Anthony Davis trade.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/team">The franchise does not control its own first-round pick again until 2031 due to trades made during the Luka Doncic era</a>. Dallas traded Davis to Washington before February's deadline, also shedding D'Angelo Russell and Jaden Hardy to create financial flexibility this summer.</p>
<p>Co-interim general managers Michael Finley and Matt Riccardi, who ran basketball operations the remainder of the season, were informed over the weekend they would not receive the permanent role. Their futures with the franchise will be determined following discussions with Ujiri.</p></description>

      <title>Magic Fire Jamahl Mosley As Head Coach</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285449/Magic-Fire-Jamahl-Mosley-As-Head-Coach</link>
      <description><p>The Orlando Magic have fired head coach Jamahl Mosley. Magic president of basketball operations Jeff Weltman will lead the search for a new head coach. Weltman signed an extension this season to continue to run Orlando's front office.</p>
<p>Mosley went 189-221 in five regular seasons as the Magic's head coach. He led Orlando to three consecutive playoff berths, but the team never advanced out of the first round.</p>
<p>Some expected the team to move on from Mosley late in the regular season, as the injury-hit Magic underachieved. Mosley rallied the team to win the eighth seed in the Play-In Tournament. Orlando then took a 3-1 lead over the top-seed Detroit Pistons, but lost three straight games to fall in the first round for the third consecutive season.</p>
<p>Mosley has been previously mentioned as a possible candidate for the New Orleans Pelicans' head coaching vacancy. </p></description>
